title: How Case Managers Can Refer a Voucher Client to Our Team
metaTitle: Refer a Section 8 Client to Our Cleveland Rental Team
metaDescription: How case managers and navigators refer a section 8 client to our rental team in Cleveland: what to send, what happens next, and how we match voucher-ready homes.
primaryKeyword: refer section 8 client rental team cleveland
answerBox: To refer a Housing Choice Voucher client to our Cleveland rental team, send us the client's bedroom size, issuing housing authority, move-in timing, and any must-be-near locations. We reply with homes that already welcome Section 8 and are HUD-inspection-ready, then arrange tours. There is no cost to refer, and the client keeps full control of which home they choose.

sections.h2: Why refer through a team that already works with voucher-ready homes
body: The hardest part of any voucher placement is rarely the paperwork — it is finding a home whose owner will actually accept the Housing Choice Voucher and pass a Housing Quality Standards (HQS) inspection. When you refer a client to us, you skip the cold-calling stage entirely. Every home we work with welcomes Section 8, so your client's search starts from **yes** instead of a stack of maybes.

We are a local rental team, not a property manager or landlord — we help renters find, tour, and apply for [Section 8-friendly homes](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/). That means you stay the client's case manager, and we simply do the legwork of matching their needs to what is available and showing them the homes.

sections.h2: What to send us when you refer a client
body: A referral can be as short as an email or a phone call. The more of the details below you can include, the faster we can come back with real options instead of follow-up questions.

items: Client's name and best contact method (with their permission to reach out)
Voucher bedroom size and the issuing housing authority (e.g. CMHA)
Household size and total bedrooms needed
Move-in timing and any voucher or inspection deadlines
Must-be-near locations — work, school, clinic, transit line, or a relative
Ground-floor, single-story, or other accessibility needs

sections.h2: What happens after you send the referral
body: Once we have the basics, we check the referral against our current selection and reply — usually the same day — with the homes that fit. From there the process is straightforward:

items: We share a short list of voucher-ready homes matching the bedroom size, timing, and area
The client (and you, if they want you there) picks which homes to see and we [book a showing](/p/book-showing)
If the client wants to move forward, they complete the standard tenant application — we walk them through it
The home's owner submits the Request for Tenancy Approval so CMHA can schedule the HQS inspection

sections.h2: How we match a client's needs to voucher-ready homes
body: Our current selection is roughly 90-plus homes concentrated on Cleveland's East and Southeast side, with additional homes in some suburbs and in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria. When you send a referral, we match on the facts that actually determine a good placement: bedroom count against the voucher size, rent within the payment standard, move-in timing, and proximity to the places the client named.

We match on needs and logistics only. In keeping with the Fair Housing Act, we never steer a client toward or away from any area based on a protected class. You can browse the kinds of homes we work with on our [houses for rent](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) and [Cleveland rentals](/cleveland-rentals/) pages so you know what to expect before you refer.

sections.h2: Local rules worth knowing before you refer
body: Cleveland itself does not yet have a source-of-income ordinance, so a landlord there can legally decline a voucher — which is exactly why referring into a pool of homes that already welcome Section 8 saves so much time. Several inner-ring suburbs do have source-of-income protections, including Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale. We welcome vouchers everywhere we operate regardless of local rule.

If your client still needs to obtain or port a voucher, the CMHA process is a separate step on their side. Our [how to apply for Section 8 with CMHA](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/how-to-apply-section-8-cleveland-cmha/) guide covers that, and you can point clients to it before or alongside a referral.

faq.q: Is there any cost to refer a client to your team?
faq.a: No. There is no fee for a case manager, navigator, or agency to refer a client, and no cost to the client for our help finding and touring homes. The renter completes the standard tenant application only once they choose a home.

faq.q: Do you need the client's permission before contacting them?
faq.a: Yes. Please confirm the client consents to us reaching out, and send their preferred contact method. We only contact clients who have agreed to hear from us.

faq.q: How quickly will I hear back after referring someone?
faq.a: Usually the same day. Because every home we work with already accepts Housing Choice Vouchers, we can match a client's bedroom size, timing, and preferred areas to what is open right now without cold-calling landlords.

faq.q: Does the client have to take a home you suggest?
faq.a: No. The client keeps full control. We share options that fit their needs, and they decide which homes to tour and which, if any, to apply for.

faq.q: How do I send a referral?
faq.a: Email support@rentfindercleveland.com or call (440) 444-4737 with the client's bedroom size, issuing housing authority, timing, and any must-be-near locations. You can also visit our housing partners page to learn more.
